title: Fermented Light Peruvian Papa Rellena
description: A comforting, fermented Peruvian dish featuring a reduction of apricot jam, perfect for a light and satisfying meal. This recipe combines the sweetness of apricots with the earthiness of potatoes, making it a delightful treat for the senses.
instructions: Scrub the potatoes clean and dry them thoroughly. Wrap each potato in foil and poke some holes in each with a fork.
Place the potatoes on a baking sheet and bake for 45-60 minutes, or until they are tender when pierced with a fork.
While the potatoes are baking, combine the dried apricots, crushed tomatoes, dill pickling spice, kosher salt, and black pepper in a saucepan.
Heat the saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is warm and fragrant.
Remove the potatoes from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Unwrap the potatoes and scoop out most of the flesh, leaving about 1/4 inch of the potato still attached to the skin.
Mash the potato flesh in a bowl with a fork until it is mostly smooth.
Add the unflavored vinegar, olive oil, and chopped cilantro to the bowl and stir to combine.
Spoon the mashed potato mixture back into the potato skins.
Drizzle the warm apricot tomato mixture over the top of each potato.
Cover the potatoes with foil and refrigerate overnight to allow the flavors to meld and the potatoes to ferment slightly.
